reason: Both answers correctly recommend migrating to managed Kubernetes, bringing in a short-term contractor to stabilize/document/execute the migration, and warn that hiring a single replacement recreates the bus-factor problem. Both note managed K8s migration is relatively low-risk since manifests/workloads carry over. However, Answer B explicitly raises the longer-term platform-simplification point ("40 microservices for 10 engineers is 4x services per person — collapsing some would cut ops load more than any hiring decision"), which is one of the key points the rubric calls out and which Answer A omits entirely. B covers this additional point while remaining just as clear and actionable as A, without meaningful loss of correctness or concision. Answer A is well organized and correct but misses the consolidation angle.
